speaker: narrator
text: Tap the text to show or hide reading controls.

speaker: narrator
text: Roaring, Jong Seohan rushed into the plum blossom forest in front of him and clenched his teeth tightly, gripping the sword in his hand.

speaker: narrator
text: It seemed that all the plum blossoms around had a mind of their own as they moved.

speaker: narrator
text: However, the flowering plum blossoms swayed back with his swing but quickly floated forwards again.

speaker: narrator
text: No matter how much force he used, he couldn’t stop their approach.

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan almost lost his mind in anger.

speaker: narrator
text: The Southern Edge-Mount Hua conference.

speaker: narrator
text: That terrible memory was still ingrained in Jong Seohan’s mind.

speaker: narrator
text: That moment when he was completely annihilated by the disciples of Mount Hua, who they had failed to see in the shadow of Mount Hua’s Divine Dragon.

speaker: narrator
text: After that day, the mood of the Southern Edge Sect changed.

speaker: narrator
text: The number of sahyungs, who were happy, decreased, and everyone became nervous.

speaker: narrator
text: A feeling of defeat had sunk into them as they had never failed before.

speaker: narrator
text: The more they did, the more Jong Seohan clung to his sword.

speaker: narrator
text: The humiliation suffered there had to be paid back in full.

speaker: narrator
text: He believed that a chance for this humiliation to be repaid would show itself to him if he religiously practiced his sword, hispropersword.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Why did this happen?’ He couldn’t understand.

speaker: narrator
text: He would have accepted this if he had neglected his training, even just once.

speaker: narrator
text: If he hadn’t given up the thoUght of ignoring Mount Hua after the last conference, he would have blamed himself and let his hopes be broken.

speaker: narrator
text: But he didn’t; he foUght with his mind and stuck to practicing.

speaker: narrator
text: Then why were such results forming?

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan clenched his teeth and swung his sword.

speaker: narrator
text: His sword wasn’t the wrong one!

speaker: narrator
text: The sword of Southern Edge could not be wrong!

speaker: narrator
text: His sword with pure white petals.

speaker: narrator
text: The pure white flowers were all blooming and a dazzling sight that was more beautiful and lively than the plum blossoms of his opponent!

speaker: narrator
text: This was a story where he pushed himself to his limits and went all in.

speaker: narrator
text: The moment he bumped into Baek Cheon’s plum blossoms, his strength was sapped, and he collapsed.

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan’s eyes began to tremble.

speaker: narrator
text: Why couldn’t he tackle those plum blossoms?

speaker: narrator
text: The Twelve Movement Snow Flower Technique was a martial art that the Souther Edge Sect’s elders had created after decades of research.

speaker: narrator
text: It was the result of them finding their own older techniques lackluster and unsatisfying.

speaker: narrator
text: And this was the same technique that had defeated the disciples of Mount Hua, which felt good for nothing.

speaker: narrator
text: This was unreasonable.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Damn it! This is nonsense!”

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan cried it out.

speaker: narrator
text: And Baek Cheon’s eyes turned cold.

speaker: narrator
text: He could hear what his opponent was saying.

speaker: narrator
text: The cry resembled the scream of Baek Cheon, who once shouted in front of Jin Geum-Ryong.

speaker: narrator
text: During these three years, their positions were changed.

speaker: narrator
text: Where did this difference come from?

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon glanced away from the stage and looked at Chung Myung, who was in his chair.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Don’t laUgh, you idiot.’ It was like Chung Myung was asking him, ‘Do you now realize how valuable the last three years were?’ Baek Cheon clenched his sword.

speaker: narrator
text: Even if they spent the same amount of time and put in the same amount of effort, it was meaningless if they didn’t go in the right direction.

speaker: narrator
text: Now, Baek Cheon had to prove that fact.

speaker: narrator
text: ThroUgh this man.

speaker: narrator
text: Beak Cheon swung his sword, which still had a subtle red glow.

speaker: narrator
text: The plum blossoms of Baek Cheon grew again and covered Jong Seohan.

speaker: narrator
text: Before he could fully comprehend what was happening, his world had turned into red petals.

speaker: narrator
text: His eyes widened with shock.

speaker: narrator
text: Did this mean he was feeling this desperate in the face of Baek Cheon, someone who wasn’t even Chung Myung?

speaker: narrator
text: Desperately swinging his sword again and again.

speaker: narrator
text: He had forgotten about his expression, and like a madman, he just kept swinging at the red plum petals, which seemed indifferent to him.

speaker: narrator
text: The plum blossom petals that flew with the warm spring breeze instantly slashed his body.

speaker: narrator
text: And as soon as they did, the plum blossoms of Mount Hua disappeared as if they had been a lie.

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan shook his head and looked at Baek Cheon.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon was sheathing his sword.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “A tree without roots will wither and die.”

speaker: narrator
text: No matter how colorful the petals were, they would still be empty.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “I don’t know if you will understand, thoUgh.”

speaker: narrator
text: Jong Seohan had fallen over.

speaker: narrator
text: A cold silence seemed to settle on everyone.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon looked at his brother.

speaker: narrator
text: Brothers who walked down different paths were now looking at each other.

speaker: narrator
text: And it wasn’t hostility.

speaker: narrator
text: A person who bears a sword must prove themselves throUgh to the end.

speaker: narrator
text: Both Baek Cheon and Jin Geum-Ryong know that in order to prove themselves, they have to defeat their opponent.

speaker: narrator
text: After a while, Baek Cheon finally turned his gaze away and started coming down the stage.

speaker: narrator
text: At the sight of a swordsman walking down draped in a black robe and with a valiant look.

speaker: narrator
text: Hot cheers poured in,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “What the hell was that?”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Like a mountain full of flowers!”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Mount Hua! Yes! It was Mount Hua! The plum blossoms of Mount Hua! I guess it was no metaphor when they say that Mount Hua’s disciples broUght plum blossoms in their swords!”

speaker: narrator
text: The cheers were strong.

speaker: narrator
text: It was a fact that everyone knew; the disciples of Mount Hua had been winning their matches till now.

speaker: narrator
text: However, it had all been done without their swords.

speaker: narrator
text: The swords of Mount Hua hadn’t been seen till now.

speaker: narrator
text: And now, Baek Cheon showed them the sword of Mount Hua, the plum blossoms within it, which was known to be the most splendid one of all.

speaker: narrator
text: So it was natural that everyone was excited.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Plum Blossom Swordsmen! Right?”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “The swordsman who learns the plum blossom sword technique of Mount Hua! A thing of the past!”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Hehe.What a funny name.”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “I don’t understand how that sect is called weak with such a technique? Isn’t this so fantastic?”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Even thoUgh prestigious sects sometimes decline, they never fully go down! Look! Isn’t it coming back strong and blooming into life?”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “It literally is blooming!haha!”

speaker: narrator
text: The people all looked at Baek Cheon and the disciples of Mount Hua with eyes full of excitement.

speaker: narrator
text: What do Murim people like?

speaker: narrator
text: Everyone has different tastes, but there were a few common things that everyone enjoyed.

speaker: narrator
text: One was the emergence of something new.

speaker: narrator
text: And the other was when a warrior of an unknown sect defeated a much better-known sect’s disciples.

speaker: narrator
text: And the last was when a sect that was in the process of crumbling found new hope and regained its name.

speaker: narrator
text: Coincidentally, right now, Mount Hua was showing all three of these things at the same time.

speaker: narrator
text: So for a moment, everyone was going crazy over Mount Hua.

speaker: narrator
text: When Baek Cheon returned, the disciples all rushed to him with excitement.

speaker: narrator
text: All of their faces were red.

speaker: narrator
text: The fact that he had overwhelmingly defeated a disciple of the Southern Edge Sect in front of so many people held special meaning for Mount Hua.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “There was nothing arrogant about it.”

speaker: narrator
text: But Baek Cheon responded slowly as if it wasn’t such a huge deal.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “The good thing is he isn’t as late as Jin Geum-Ryong.”

speaker: narrator
text: He had to defeat Jin Geum-Ryong to defeat the Southern Edge Sect.

speaker: narrator
text: The disciples of Mount Hua all seemed happy.

speaker: narrator
text: AlthoUgh being their great sahyung, only now did he get to do his role properly?

speaker: narrator
text: Watching Baek Cheon break down Jong Seohan, even the slightest doubts these other disciples had were blown away.

speaker: narrator
text: If Baek Cheon broke Jin Geum-Ryong like this, Mount Hua would never be swayed again by the name of Southern Edge.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “The feud ends here. After this competition, the Southern Edge will no longer be a huge name for Mount Hua.”

speaker: narrator
text: Jin Geum-Ryong glared at the disciples of Mount Hua with cold eyes.

speaker: narrator
text: Not Dong-Ryong but Baek Cheon.

speaker: narrator
text: The name of the person who used to be his brother was now his enemy.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon didn’t suit his actions.

speaker: narrator
text: Pretending to be confident but trembling in fear was what suited him.

speaker: narrator
text: Jin Geum-Ryong took his gaze away.

speaker: narrator
text: All the sajaes had gloomy eyes.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Straighten your shoulders.”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Do not show your disappointment to the people of Mount Hua. Are you afraid that something bad will happen? Still, you people are the disciples of the Great Southern Edge Sect!”

speaker: narrator
text: Everyone felt a chill run throUgh them at those words and forcibly straightened their shoulders.

speaker: baek cheon
text: “The loss was unexpected, but it changes nothing. In the end, all I need to do is win.”

speaker: narrator
text: Jin Geum-Ryong’s gaze turned to Baek Cheon again, and his eyes looked colder when he saw that expressionless face.

speaker: narrator
text: At first, he didn’t care about Baek Cheon.

speaker: narrator
text: There was growth, but he didn’t think it was something special.

speaker: narrator
text: And since they shared the same blood, he didn’t care.

speaker: narrator
text: He was a weak kid who didn’t do anything properly because he had gone to Mount Hua.

speaker: narrator
text: The problem wasn’t Baek Cheon even now.

speaker: narrator
text: Jin Geum-Ryong’s eyes turned to Chung Myung, who was giggling in front of the gambling table.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘You have time to do that?’ Jin Geum-Ryong mumbled,

speaker: chung myung
text: “Don’t worry. I will defeat Mount Hua’s Divine Dragon and restore the honor of the Southern Edge Sect. And then those stupid people will know who the real heroes are.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Of course, sahyung!”

speaker: narrator
text: Half faith and half fear.

speaker: narrator
text: Those soulless words weren’t really taken in by Jin Geum-Ryong.

speaker: narrator
text: After all, it was enoUgh to prove them with results.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Why aren’t you saying anything?”

speaker: narrator
text: At Jin Geum-Ryong’s question, Lee Song-Baek raised his head.

speaker: narrator
text: Jin Geum-Ryong looked at him, who appeared silent the whole time.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘He doesn’t mess around with me.’

speaker: narrator
text: And he had a face like he wanted to say something.

speaker: chung myung
text: “I don’t know what you are asking.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Do you think I can defeat Mount Hua’s Divine Dragon?”

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek glanced back at Chung Myung and then looked back at his sahyung,

speaker: chung myung
text: “The opponent for you is your younger brother.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Can someone who doesn’t see their opponent in front of him aim to be bigger?”

speaker: narrator
text: At his words, the younger disciples were enraged,

speaker: chung myung
text: “How dare you say that to our sahyung!”

speaker: narrator
text: Jin Geum-Ryong raised his hand and stopped them, And he looked at Lee Song-Baek coldly,

speaker: chung myung
text: “He will know later… if I am right or wrong.”

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek only bowed his head.

speaker: narrator
text: The sharp eyes of his sahyungs didn’t leave him for a long time.

speaker: narrator
text: He simply sighed, Talks done with hotheads couldn’t really be called talks.

speaker: narrator
text: The Southern Edge Sect had already lost the time it had; it was now narrow-minded and unable to sympathize with others.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Disciple Chung Myung.

speaker: narrator
text: What do you think?’ He looked at Chung Myung.

speaker: chung myung
text: “This spar was won by Disciple Baek Cheon of Mount Hua. Those who bet on Baek Cheon, come and take your money!”

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung ran towards them.

speaker: narrator
text: Wei Lishan smiled and took the token from Chung Myung.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Let’s see. Disciple gave us 10,000 nyangs….”

speaker: narrator
text: Some of the money collected by Wei Lishan was pulled back, and the rest was all pushed to Chung Myung.

speaker: narrator
text: Since Chung Myung had given the highest stakes there, he got the largest cut.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung flicked some back to Wei Lishan.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Ah, no, you don’t have to give…”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Eh.If I take all of this, I will get a stomach ache. Take some.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “I am thankful then. But how am I supposed to take….”

speaker: narrator
text: Before Wei Lishan could finish speaking, Chung Myung took something from his arms and unfolded it, He took a large cloth sack, and Wei Lishan opened his mouth.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung started to sweep the remaining money into it.

speaker: narrator
text: He pushed the gold, silver, and any money slips into the sack and tied it.

speaker: narrator
text: And suddenly turned his head, Jo Gul, who was looking at Chung Myung, flinched.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung threw the sack at him.

speaker: narrator
text: Surprised by the heaviness, Jo Gul flinched.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Yah!What is this supposed to mean?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Leave it in the middle of our group! No one can steal it then!”

speaker: chung myung
text: “This is just the beginning for us.”

speaker: narrator
text: I need to rob all the people in here!

speaker: chung myung
text: “Wow, that is good. You got all that?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “At the end of the day, it doubled my money. I had put up ten thousand.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “You say you gave ten thousand at once and broUght more back?”

speaker: narrator
text: Greed grew in the eyes of people who noticed.

speaker: narrator
text: They realized that this wasn’t small gambling.

speaker: narrator
text: Even those who weren’t too interested in the sight of small Chung Myung, with his huge sack, became interested when they heard how much was in it.

speaker: narrator
text: Wei Lishan cleared his throat,

speaker: chung myung
text: “Let’s start! This time, it is Mok Oh of the Beggars union and Mount Hua’s Jo Gul…”

speaker: chung myung
text: “10,000 for Mount Hua’s Jo Gul.”

speaker: narrator
text: Another bet for 10,000.

speaker: narrator
text: All the gamblers there were shocked.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung, who had placed it, asked,

speaker: chung myung
text: “What? You won’t take the bet?”

speaker: narrator
text: As soon as his words fell, the others began to pounce in like they were starving.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Didn’t you lose a lot earlier?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Don’t be stupid! I can win it all back at once! I might get even more! No, I can earn dozens of it!”

speaker: narrator
text: Seeing this chaos, Chung Myung smiled,

speaker: chung myung
text: “Making money will be so easy.”

speaker: narrator
text: He wished such competitions were held more often, then he would be the richest person in the world.

speaker: narrator
text: Seeing Chung Myung drenched in his own dreams, Jo Gul shook his head.

speaker: narrator
text: And now was his time to prepare, and Baek Cheon summoned him, When Jo Gul turned to Baek Cheon, the man had a serious expression,

speaker: chung myung
text: “Do not underestimate your opponent. They aren’t an easy one to beat. Do your best to win.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Are you saying to use the Plum Blossom Sword Technique?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Use it if you have to.”

speaker: narrator
text: He did his best to defeat the opponent, and like Baek Cheon, he got cheered on by the crowd…

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Sahyung! Sahyung! I bet my money on sahyung! You knew you would die if you lost, right?!”

speaker: narrator
text: Not you!

speaker: narrator
text: Not you, you brat!

speaker: narrator
text: Stop cheering like that!

speaker: narrator
text: Return of the Mount Hua Sect
